title: quercus depressa
intro: Quercus depressa, also known as the blue scrub oak, is a small shrub-like evergreen tree native to the southwestern United States. It is a species of oak tree known for its medicinal properties, particularly its tannins and polyphenols, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Quercus depressa can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, and are often used to support digestive health and immune function.
